primary_alignment: Border-first pragmatists who sell passage and warnings to anyone who respects their lines.
secondary_alignment_notes: Loose Autark and survivalist leanings; loyalty follows whoever funds fuel, water, and extraction rights, not dogma.

Camarilla.notes: Will escort Camarilla envoys along safe routes if debts are honored; defy any edict that erases desert warnings.

Anarchs.notes: Share distrust of hierarchy but refuse reckless runs that draw Garou or mortal troopers toward Gangrel camps.

Brujah.notes: Brujah muscle takes Gangrel route intel seriously; Gangrel expect backup when escorts go loud.

Nosferatu.notes: Trade sewer and canal maps for desert trail intel; uphold shared quiet around covert ingress points.

Malkavian.notes: Avoid inviting prophetic wanderers outside the lights; heed their warnings about nights when the sand hums.

Garou.notes: Treat the Garou-held wilds as a lethal buffer; negotiate only to keep roads marginally usable and avoid provoking hunts.

Other_Clans.notes: Provide escort at cost; deny passage to anyone who burns safehouses or ignores posted ‘no-go’ signs.

external: Borders harden after the Prince’s murder; every domain needs trusted couriers who can bypass new checkpoints.
Garou presence constrains expansion; the wild is not neutral territory but an active threat shaping city policy.
internal: Route knowledge is currency; those who disrespect it lose escort privileges.
Neutrality is earned through consistent payment, not declared by rhetoric.
Survival obligations override sect etiquette when travel becomes lethal.

territorial_boundaries: The canal maintenance roads east of Sky Harbor used as silent night corridors; off-limits to firefights.
I-10 westbound rest stops after midnight treated as Gangrel-controlled muster points.
Salt River dry beds south of the city marked with cairns indicating Garou patrol proximity.
Truck spurs near the Black Canyon Highway leveraged as evacuation staging, guarded against trespass.
flashpoints: Camarilla patrols attempt to extend city law beyond the loop, colliding with Gangrel warning markers.
Anarch raiders use desert frontage roads, drawing Garou attention and threatening all nocturnal travel.
Smuggled kine cross a marked wash on a red night, prompting Gangrel to decide between rescue or lesson.
Nosferatu tapping new fiber trenches near reservation edges risk breaching Garou taboos.
A blood hunt decree demands pursuit past the city line, forcing a choice between sect obedience and survival rules.
